race.comment: Testing way to kick off the carnival and might be able to start off with a value play. ROYAL OVERTURE (13) started $31 on debut over 1412m at Echuca and it was only in the last furlong that she felt the pinch. With that under the belt, and back in trip, she could take some running down, hopefully at a nice price again. MENZIES CREEK (5) is a seven-start maiden who looks close to breaking the duck after finishing in the placings at his last couple of runs, most recently when 4 lengths away in second over 1300m at Stawell. Blinkers come off now and draws beautifully. PIERRE LE QUILL (6) missed the placings first up recently but was not too far from the winner when fourth over 1109m at Echuca. Can go on with it. TIJOLA (9) was out of the money when resuming from a spell last start over 1100m at Werribee, beaten 3 lengths. Fitter now and nicely placed to play a pivotal role.
